dimensions.name: ac-coverage
dimensions.evidence: All 11 ACs in definition.yaml (repair-deliverables-cli) and spec AC 1-11 have implementation evidence. AC1 (exit 0 on valid manifest): TestRowRepairDeliverables_HappyPath passes. AC2 (schema enforces required fields): validateRepairManifest() at row_repair.go:273-308 enforces name/status/commit/evidence_paths. AC3 (atomic update + audit): writeJSONMapAtomic at row_repair.go:247 + appendAuditEntry at row_repair.go:238. AC4 (refuses non-archived without --force-active): row_repair.go:107-113, tested by TestRowRepairDeliverables_NonArchivedWithoutForceActive. AC5 (refuses conflict without --replace, names deliverable): row_repair.go:171-176, message format verified in TestRowRepairDeliverables_ConflictWithoutReplace. AC6 (schema at canonical path): schemas/repair-deliverables-manifest.schema.json exists. AC7 (unit tests): 10 TestXxx functions covering all required scenarios. AC8 (thin shim): bin/rws:2955-2961, pure delegation with exec. AC9 (partial-repair semantics): row_repair.go:159-176 + 183-186, tested by TestRowRepairDeliverables_PartialRepair. AC10 (empty manifest fails): TestRowRepairDeliverables_EmptyDeliverables exit 4. AC11 (distinct exit codes 2 vs 3): row_repair.go:82-85 (exit 2) vs row_repair.go:89-94 (exit 3). Minor: definition.yaml AC#2 says 'evidence_path' (singular) — a typo in the definition; spec.md and implementation correctly use 'evidence_paths' (plural). Not held against implementation.

dimensions.name: spec-fidelity
dimensions.evidence: CLI surface matches spec exactly: positional + --manifest (required) + --force-active + --replace + --json (row_repair.go:36-39). parseArgs call matches spec verbatim (row_repair.go:36-39 vs spec CLI surface table). Exit code catalog matches spec table: 0/1/2/3/4/5/6 all implemented correctly. Manifest YAML/JSON parsing via gopkg.in/yaml.v3 as specified. Sidecar JSONL at .furrow/rows/<name>/repair-audit.jsonl (row_repair.go:313). Audit entry shape (timestamp, manifest, commit, decided_by, entries_added, entries_skipped) matches spec example (row_repair.go:230-237). Top-level commit used as default when per-entry commit is empty (row_repair.go:192-195). decided_by defaults to 'manual' (row_repair.go:145-147). One minor spec discrepancy: the spec's Go function signature block says 'returns error' but the explanatory text clarifies it returns int. Implementation correctly returns int (row_repair.go:35), consistent with every other run* function. Not a real divergence — the spec note overrides the misleading stub signature. app.go case at line 96-97 matches spec exactly. bin/rws shim position (after diff, before focus) matches spec insertion point at line 2955.

dimensions.name: test-rigor
dimensions.evidence: Scenario coverage is strong: happy path (TestRowRepairDeliverables_HappyPath), missing manifest (TestRowRepairDeliverables_MissingManifest exit 3), schema-invalid manifest (TestRowRepairDeliverables_SchemaInvalid_MissingEvidencePaths exit 4), empty deliverables (TestRowRepairDeliverables_EmptyDeliverables exit 4), conflict without --replace (TestRowRepairDeliverables_ConflictWithoutReplace exit 5), conflict with --replace (TestRowRepairDeliverables_ConflictWithReplace), non-existent row (TestRowRepairDeliverables_NonExistentRow exit 2), partial repair (TestRowRepairDeliverables_PartialRepair), non-archived without --force-active (TestRowRepairDeliverables_NonArchivedWithoutForceActive exit 1), non-archived with --force-active (TestRowRepairDeliverables_NonArchivedWithForceActive). All 10 scenarios pass. HOWEVER: (1) Spec AC#7 explicitly requires 'table-driven tests' and the commit message claims 'Ten table-driven test scenarios' — the implementation uses 10 individual TestXxx functions, not the Go table-driven pattern (slice of structs with t.Run). This violates the spec AC#7 and project Go conventions (CLAUDE.md: 'Table-driven tests'). (2) readRepairAuditLast helper (row_repair_test.go:387-410) is defined but never called — dead code in the test file. (3) No test covers the usage error path (missing row-name positional, exit 1). Not a disqualifying gap since AC#7 lists specific scenarios and that one is not among them, but the table-driven structure requirement is explicitly called out in the spec and is not met.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: Error wrapping uses %w throughout appendAuditEntry (row_repair.go:316, 319, 323). No swallowed errors — all errors propagate to a.fail() or are returned. No provider-side interfaces. No public API returning `any`. cliError struct pattern follows existing conventions. defer func() { _ = f.Close() }() at row_repair.go:322 correctly discards Close error for append-only file. Naming follows project snake_case/kebab-case conventions. Import block is clean. No bespoke temp logic — reuses writeJSONMapAtomic. One note: audit write (line 238) happens before state.json write (line 247); if writeJSONMapAtomic fails, the audit JSONL gains an orphaned entry whose corresponding state change did not land. The spec does not explicitly require the reverse ordering, and the sidecar is 'purely for audit/forensic use', so this is acceptable but worth noting. go vet passes clean. go build passes.

dimensions.name: schema-correctness
dimensions.evidence: schemas/repair-deliverables-manifest.schema.json uses $schema: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema. Top-level required: ['deliverables'] and additionalProperties: false. deliverables array has minItems: 1. Each deliverable item: required ['name','status','commit','evidence_paths'], additionalProperties: false. name and commit have minLength: 1. status has enum ['not_started','in_progress','completed','blocked']. evidence_paths array has minItems: 1; each item: required ['path'], additionalProperties: false, path has minLength: 1, lines and note are optional strings. Schema matches the Go inline validation in validateRepairManifest() (row_repair.go:273-308) exactly. The spec example manifest would pass this schema. No duplicate schema files found. Schema is valid JSON (file parses cleanly).

dimensions.name: atomicity
dimensions.evidence: State.json mutations use writeJSONMapAtomic (row_repair.go:247) which writes to .state-*.tmp in the same directory then calls os.Rename (util.go:74-90). No partial-write windows exist for state.json. All pre-write checks (schema, conflict, missing row) return before touching state.json. The only atomicity caveat is the sidecar audit file (appendAuditEntry), which uses O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY and is not covered by the temp-rename pattern — but the spec explicitly calls the sidecar 'not read by the harness for state-driven decisions' and specifies it as append-only, so single-line appends are safe. No direct state.json edits bypass writeJSONMapAtomic.

dimensions.name: shim-cleanliness
dimensions.evidence: bin/rws:2955-2961: the repair-deliverables case sets FURROW_BIN via ${FURROW_BIN:-${FURROW_ROOT}/bin/furrow} (supports override for test environments per spec), does a binary-exists check (die if not executable), then exec's to the Go binary preserving exit codes. No validation logic, no state reads, no output transformation. Insertion point is after diff) (line 2954) and before focus) (line 2962), matching spec insertion point. FURROW_ROOT is confirmed set at bin/rws:10 before this case is reached. Pattern matches spec's recommended shim pattern exactly.

dimensions.name: constraint-compliance
dimensions.evidence: state.schema.json was not modified in commit d8d3041 (confirmed via git show --name-only). No new external Go dependencies: gopkg.in/yaml.v3 was already in go.mod (confirmed: 'require gopkg.in/yaml.v3 v3.0.1'). Conventional commit prefix is 'feat(cli):' — valid. Manifest schema is additive (new file only). One unplanned file: .furrow/almanac/roadmap.yaml was modified in commit d8d3041 (2 lines changed), which is not in repair-deliverables-cli's file_ownership. The roadmap.yaml change is a depends_on correction (work/handoff-and-context-routing → work/orchestration-delegation-contract) and appears incidental/bookkeeping. The definition.yaml constraint 'Manifest schema is additive — does not modify state.schema.json' is satisfied. The unplanned roadmap.yaml change is minor and not a correctness concern, but is technically outside file_ownership scope.

notes:
The implementation is functionally correct and well-structured. All 11 acceptance criteria have clear implementation evidence, all 10 test scenarios pass, the schema is well-formed, atomicity is properly handled via writeJSONMapAtomic, and the bin/rws shim is a clean pure-delegation pattern. The code follows Go conventions with proper error wrapping, no swallowed errors, and clean import structure.

The sole FAIL is test-rigor, driven by a specific spec violation: AC#7 explicitly requires 'table-driven tests' and the commit message claims this, but the implementation uses 10 individual TestXxx functions rather than the Go table-driven pattern ([]struct{...} with t.Run subtest dispatch). The project CLAUDE.md also mandates 'Table-driven tests' as a Go convention. Additionally, the readRepairAuditLast helper (row_repair_test.go:387-410) is defined but never called anywhere in the test suite — dead code that should either be used or removed. Two minor issues do not affect the FAIL verdict but are worth noting: the roadmap.yaml modification is outside the deliverable's file_ownership (unplanned change, low risk); and the spec's Go function signature stub misleadingly says 'returns error' when the implementation correctly returns int (the spec's own explanatory text clarifies this and the implementation matches all other run* functions).
